---
title: "Research"
---

## Overview

My research focuses on learning visual representations efficiently and reliably in settings where labels, memory, or compute are limited. I am especially interested in methods that remain useful under realistic constraints rather than only in ideal benchmark settings.

## Research Areas

### Label-Efficient Learning

I am interested in approaches that reduce annotation cost while preserving downstream usefulness. This includes weak supervision, semi-supervised learning, and representation learning strategies that can extract more value from limited labels.

### Scene Understanding

A recurring theme in my work is how to build models that understand structured visual scenes. Semantic segmentation and related dense prediction tasks are a natural testbed for this goal.

### Object Localization and Dense Prediction

I care about models that can localize and segment meaningful visual regions accurately while remaining practical to train and deploy.

## Ongoing Direction

Recent work has included storage-efficient vision training and practical representation learning settings. As this site evolves, this page can grow into a more detailed research narrative with figures, papers, and project links.
